title: Administration of Justice Expenses Act
text:
ADMINISTRATION OF JUSTICE EXPENSES [CH.61 – 1
ADMINISTRATION OF JUSTICE EXPENSES
CHAPTER 61
ADMINISTRATION OF JUSTICE EXPENSES
ARRANGEMENT OF SECTIONS
SECTION
1. Short title.
2. Expenses in connection with the due administration of justice authorised.
–––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––
[Original Service 2001] STATUTE LAW OF THE BAHAMAS

ADMINISTRATION OF JUSTICE EXPENSES [CH.61 – 3
CHAPTER 61
ADMINISTRATION OF JUSTICE EXPENSES
An Act to authorise payment of certain expenses in 9 of 1897
connection with the due administration of justice. 7 of 1924
E.L.A.O., 1974
[Commencement 14th April, 1897]
1. This Act may be cited as the Administration of Short title.
Justice Expenses Act.
2. Whenever in the opinion of the Attorney-General it may Expenses in con-
be necessary in furtherance of the ends of justice to incur any expense nection with the
due administra-
in the employment of professional men, or in payment of rewards, or
tion of justice
otherwise, it shall be lawful for the Governor-General by warrant in authorised
the usual manner to direct the payment out of the Consolidated Fund 7 of 1924, s. 2;
of all expenses incurred for any of the purposes aforesaid: E.L.A.O., 1974
Provided that a Stipendiary and Circuit Magistrate
may when he considers the circumstances so require
engage the services of an interpreter or interpreters without
reference to the Attorney-General.
